1→use schemars::JsonSchema;
     2→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     3→use serde_json::Value;
     4→
     5→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
     6→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
     7→pub enum Role {
     8→    System,
     9→    User,
    10→    Assistant,
    11→    Tool,
    12→}
    13→
    14→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
    15→pub struct ChatMessage {
    16→    pub role: Role,
    17→    pub content: String,
    18→    /// For tool result messages, the ID of the tool call this responds to.
    19→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    20→    pub tool_call_id: Option<String>,
    21→}
    22→
    23→impl ChatMessage {
    24→    pub fn system(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    25→        Self {
    26→            role: Role::System,
    27→            content: content.into(),
    28→            tool_call_id: None,
    29→        }
    30→    }
    31→
    32→    pub fn user(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    33→        Self {
    34→            role: Role::User,
    35→            content: content.into(),
    36→            tool_call_id: None,
    37→        }
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    pub fn assistant(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    41→        Self {
    42→            role: Role::Assistant,
    43→            content: content.into(),
    44→            tool_call_id: None,
    45→        }
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    pub fn tool(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    49→        Self {
    50→            role: Role::Tool,
    51→            content: content.into(),
    52→            tool_call_id: None,
    53→        }
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    pub fn tool_result(call_id: impl Into<String>, content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    57→        Self {
    58→            role: Role::Tool,
    59→            content: content.into(),
    60→            tool_call_id: Some(call_id.into()),
    61→        }
    62→    }
    63→}
    64→
    65→/// MCP-compatible behavioral annotations for tools.
    66→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
    67→pub struct ToolAnnotations {
    68→    /// Tool does not modify its environment.
    69→    #[serde(default)]
    70→    pub read_only: bool,
    71→    /// Tool may perform destructive updates.
    72→    #[serde(default)]
    73→    pub destructive: bool,
    74→    /// Repeated calls with same args produce same result.
    75→    #[serde(default)]
    76→    pub idempotent: bool,
    77→    /// Tool interacts with external entities (network, APIs).
    78→    #[serde(default)]
    79→    pub open_world: bool,
    80→    /// Tool requires user confirmation before execution.
    81→    #[serde(default)]
    82→    pub requires_confirmation: bool,
    83→}
    84→
    85→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
    86→pub struct ToolDefinition {
    87→    pub name: String,
    88→    pub description: String,
    89→    pub input_schema: Value,
    90→
    91→    // ── MCP-aligned fields (all optional, backward-compatible) ──
    92→    /// Human-readable display name (MCP: title).
    93→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    94→    pub title: Option<String>,
    95→    /// JSON Schema for structured output (MCP: outputSchema).
    96→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    97→    pub output_schema: Option<Value>,
    98→    /// Behavioral hints (MCP: annotations).
    99→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   100→    pub annotations: Option<ToolAnnotations>,
   101→
   102→    // ── Arcan extensions ──
   103→    /// Tool category for grouping ("filesystem", "code", "shell", "mcp").
   104→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   105→    pub category: Option<String>,
   106→    /// Tags for filtering and matching (skills.sh compatible).
   107→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   108→    pub tags: Vec<String>,
   109→    /// Maximum execution timeout in seconds.
   110→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   111→    pub timeout_secs: Option<u32>,
   112→}
   113→
   114→/// Typed content block in a tool result (MCP-compatible).
   115→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   116→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   117→pub enum ToolContent {
   118→    Text { text: String },
   119→    Image { data: String, mime_type: String },
   120→    Json { value: Value },
   121→}
   122→
   123→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   124→pub struct ToolCall {
   125→    pub call_id: String,
   126→    pub tool_name: String,
   127→    #[serde(default)]
   128→    pub input: Value,
   129→}
   130→
   131→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   132→pub struct ToolResult {
   133→    pub call_id: String,
   134→    pub tool_name: String,
   135→    #[serde(default)]
   136→    pub output: Value,
   137→    /// MCP-style typed content blocks (optional, alongside output for compat).
   138→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   139→    pub content: Option<Vec<ToolContent>>,
   140→    /// Whether this result represents an error (MCP: isError).
   141→    #[serde(default)]
   142→    pub is_error: bool,
   143→    pub state_patch: Option<StatePatch>,
   144→}
   145→
   146→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   147→pub struct ToolResultSummary {
   148→    pub call_id: String,
   149→    pub tool_name: String,
   150→    #[serde(default)]
   151→    pub output: Value,
   152→}
   153→
   154→impl From<&ToolResult> for ToolResultSummary {
   155→    fn from(value: &ToolResult) -> Self {
   156→        Self {
   157→            call_id: value.call_id.clone(),
   158→            tool_name: value.tool_name.clone(),
   159→            output: value.output.clone(),
   160→        }
   161→    }
   162→}
   163→
   164→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   165→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   166→pub enum StatePatchFormat {
   167→    JsonPatch,
   168→    MergePatch,
   169→}
   170→
   171→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   172→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   173→pub enum StatePatchSource {
   174→    Model,
   175→    Tool,
   176→    System,
   177→}
   178→
   179→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   180→pub struct StatePatch {
   181→    pub format: StatePatchFormat,
   182→    #[serde(default)]
   183→    pub patch: Value,
   184→    pub source: StatePatchSource,
   185→}
   186→
   187→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   188→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   189→pub enum ModelStopReason {
   190→    EndTurn,
   191→    ToolUse,
   192→    NeedsUser,
   193→    MaxTokens,
   194→    Safety,
   195→    Unknown,
   196→}
   197→
   198→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   199→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   200→pub enum ModelDirective {
   201→    Text { delta: String },
   202→    ToolCall { call: ToolCall },
   203→    StatePatch { patch: StatePatch },
   204→    FinalAnswer { text: String },
   205→}
   206→
   207→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   208→pub struct ModelTurn {
   209→    pub directives: Vec<ModelDirective>,
   210→    pub stop_reason: ModelStopReason,
   211→    /// Token usage for this turn (if reported by the provider).
   212→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   213→    pub usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   214→}
   215→
   216→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   217→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   218→pub struct TokenUsage {
   219→    /// Tokens in the input/prompt.
   220→    #[serde(default)]
   221→    pub input_tokens: u64,
   222→    /// Tokens in the output/completion.
   223→    #[serde(default)]
   224→    pub output_tokens: u64,
   225→    /// Tokens from cache reads (Anthropic-specific).
   226→    #[serde(default)]
   227→    pub cache_read_tokens: u64,
   228→    /// Tokens written to cache (Anthropic-specific).
   229→    #[serde(default)]
   230→    pub cache_creation_tokens: u64,
   231→}
   232→
   233→impl TokenUsage {
   234→    /// Accumulate another usage into this one.
   235→    pub fn accumulate(&mut self, other: &TokenUsage) {
   236→        self.input_tokens += other.input_tokens;
   237→        self.output_tokens += other.output_tokens;
   238→        self.cache_read_tokens += other.cache_read_tokens;
   239→        self.cache_creation_tokens += other.cache_creation_tokens;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    pub fn total(&self) -> u64 {
   243→        self.input_tokens + self.output_tokens
   244→    }
   245→}
   246→
   247→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   248→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   249→pub enum RunStopReason {
   250→    Completed,
   251→    NeedsUser,
   252→    BlockedByPolicy,
   253→    BudgetExceeded,
   254→    Cancelled,
   255→    Error,
   256→}
   257→
   258→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   259→#[serde(tag = "part_type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   260→pub enum AgentEvent {
   261→    RunStarted {
   262→        run_id: String,
   263→        session_id: String,
   264→        provider: String,
   265→        max_iterations: u32,
   266→    },
   267→    IterationStarted {
   268→        run_id: String,
   269→        session_id: String,
   270→        iteration: u32,
   271→    },
   272→    ModelOutput {
   273→        run_id: String,
   274→        session_id: String,
   275→        iteration: u32,
   276→        stop_reason: ModelStopReason,
   277→        directive_count: usize,
   278→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   279→        usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   280→    },
   281→    TextDelta {
   282→        run_id: String,
   283→        session_id: String,
   284→        iteration: u32,
   285→        delta: String,
   286→    },

   289→        session_id: String,

   291→        call: ToolCall,
   292→    },
   293→    ToolCallCompleted {
   294→        run_id: String,
   295→        session_id: String,
   296→        iteration: u32,
   297→        result: ToolResultSummary,
   298→    },
   299→    ToolCallFailed {
   300→        run_id: String,
   301→        session_id: String,
   302→        iteration: u32,
   303→        call_id: String,
   304→        tool_name: String,
   305→        error: String,
   306→    },
   307→    StatePatched {
   308→        run_id: String,
   309→        session_id: String,
   310→        iteration: u32,
   311→        patch: StatePatch,
   312→        revision: u64,
   313→    },
   314→    ContextCompacted {
   315→        run_id: String,
   316→        session_id: String,
   317→        iteration: u32,
   318→        dropped_count: usize,
   319→        tokens_before: usize,
   320→        tokens_after: usize,
   321→    },
   322→    ApprovalRequested {
   323→        run_id: String,
   324→        session_id: String,
   325→        approval_id: String,
   326→        call_id: String,
   327→        tool_name: String,
   328→        arguments: serde_json::Value,
   329→        /// Risk level as string ("low"/"medium"/"high"/"critical") to keep arcan-core lago-free.
   330→        risk: String,

   332→    ApprovalResolved {
   333→        run_id: String,

   350→        final_answer: Option<String>,
   351→    },
   352→}

   355→    pub fn as_sse_data(&self) -> Result<String, serde_json::Error> {

   357→        Ok(format!("data: {payload}\n\n"))
   358→    }
   359→}

content.text:
## Analysis of arcan-spaces Bridge Crate

I've read all the requested files. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the arcan-spaces bridge architecture:

---

## 1. The SpacesPort Trait (port.rs)

The `SpacesPort` trait is the core abstraction that defines a synchronous interface for Spaces operations:

**Bridge-local types** (mirroring Spaces WASM module, no direct SDK dependency):
- `SpacesChannelType`: Text, Voice, Announcement, AgentLog
- `SpacesChannel`: Channel metadata (id, server_id, name, type, description)
- `SpacesMessageType`: Text, System, Join, Leave, AgentEvent
- `SpacesMessage`: Channel message with sender, content, timestamps, threading
- `SpacesDirectMessage`: DM with sender, recipient, content

**Core port methods** (all synchronous to match Arcan's `Tool` trait contract):
- `send_message(channel_id, content, thread_id?, reply_to_id?)` → `SpacesMessage`
- `list_channels(server_id)` → `Vec<SpacesChannel>`
- `read_messages(channel_id, limit, before_id?)` → `Vec<SpacesMessage>`
- `send_dm(recipient, content)` → `SpacesDirectMessage`

**Key design decision**: The trait is synchronous. Concrete implementations using the async SpacetimeDB SDK must handle async-to-sync bridging internally (likely via `spawn_blocking`).

---

## 2. Tool Definitions (tools.rs)

Four tool implementations, all implementing Arcan's `Tool` trait:

### **SpacesSendMessageTool**
- **Name**: `spaces_send_message`
- **Category**: `spaces`
- **Tags**: `spaces`, `messaging`
- **Annotations**: Not read-only, not destructive, not idempotent, open-world, no confirmation required
- **Input**: `channel_id` (required), `content` (required, max 4000 chars), `thread_id` (optional), `reply_to_id` (optional)
- **Validation**: Content must not be empty, max 4000 characters

### **SpacesListChannelsTool**
- **Name**: `spaces_list_channels`
- **Category**: `spaces`
- **Annotations**: Read-only, idempotent, open-world
- **Input**: `server_id` (default: 1)
- **Returns**: Array of channels with metadata

### **SpacesReadMessagesTool**
- **Name**: `spaces_read_messages`
- **Category**: `spaces`
- **Annotations**: Read-only, idempotent, open-world
- **Input**: `channel_id` (required), `limit` (default 50, max 200), `before_id` (optional for pagination)
- **Returns**: Array of messages

### **SpacesSendDmTool**
- **Name**: `spaces_send_dm`
- **Category**: `spaces`
- **Annotations**: Not read-only, not idempotent, open-world
- **Input**: `recipient` (hex string, required), `content` (required, max 4000 chars)
- **Validation**: Recipient must not be empty, content validated same as send_message

**Shared validation pattern**:
- Content length enforcement (max 4000 chars)
- Empty content rejection
- Consistent error conversion from `SpacesBridgeError` → `CoreError::ToolExecution`

---

## 3. Middleware (middleware.rs)

### **SpacesActivityMiddleware**
Implements Arcan's `Middleware` trait to log agent activity to a Spaces AgentLog channel.

**Constructor**: `new(port: Arc<dyn SpacesPort>, agent_log_channel_id: u64)`

**Hooks**:
1. **`post_tool_call`**: Logs tool execution status to the agent-log channel
   - Format: `[agent] Tool '<tool_name>' completed/failed`
   - Non-blocking: Port errors are swallowed (logged via `tracing::warn!`)
   
2. **`on_run_finished`**: Logs run completion summaries
   - Format: `[agent] Run '<run_id>' finished: <reason> (iterations: <count>)`
   - Counts iterations from `AgentEvent::IterationStarted` events

**Critical pattern**: Observability MUST NEVER block the agent loop. All port failures are swallowed and logged as warnings.

---

## 4. Mock Hub (mock.rs)

### **MockSpacesClient**
Thread-safe in-memory implementation of `SpacesPort` for testing.

**State** (using interior mutability):
- `channels: Mutex<Vec<SpacesChannel>>`
- `messages: Mutex<Vec<SpacesMessage>>`
- `dms: Mutex<Vec<SpacesDirectMessage>>`
- `next_id: AtomicU64` (deterministic ID generation)
- `force_error: Mutex<Option<String>>` (error injection for tests)

**Default hub** (`default_hub()`):
Pre-seeded with 3 channels:
1. `#general` (Text)
2. `#agent-logs` (AgentLog)
3. `#system` (Announcement)

**Features**:
- Validates channel existence before sending messages
- Filters by server_id when listing channels
- Filters by channel_id and before_id when reading messages
- Deterministic IDs starting from 100
- Error injection support for testing failure paths

**Test utilities**:
- `sent_messages()` → All messages sent
- `sent_dms()` → All DMs sent
- `message_count()`, `dm_count()` → Counts

---

## 5. Error Handling (error.rs)

### **SpacesBridgeError**
Defined using `thiserror`:

- `Connection(String)` — Network/SDK errors
- `ChannelNotFound(String)` — Invalid channel ID
- `ServerNotFound(String)` — Invalid server ID
- `RecipientNotFound(String)` — Invalid DM recipient
- `Validation(String)` — Input validation failures
- `ReducerFailed(String)` — SpacetimeDB reducer execution failures

**Conversion**: `into_core_error(&tool_name)` → `CoreError::ToolExecution` for consistent error surfacing in tools.

---

## 6. Crate Configuration (Cargo.toml)

**Dependencies**:
- `arcan-core` (workspace path + version)
- `serde`, `serde_json` (workspace)
- `thiserror`, `tracing` (workspace)

**No direct SpacetimeDB SDK dependency** — abstraction is protocol-level, not SDK-level. This keeps the bridge decoupled and allows for:
- Easy mocking (no async SDK in tests)
- Flexibility to swap SDK versions
- Clean separation between Arcan's sync tool contract and Spaces' async SDK

---

## 7. Integration in Arcan Binary (main.rs)

**Registration** (lines 486-491):
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "spaces")]
{
    let spaces_port: Arc<dyn arcan_spaces::SpacesPort> =
        Arc::new(arcan_spaces::MockSpacesClient::default_hub());
    arcan_spaces::register_spaces_tools(&mut registry, spaces_port);
}
```

**How it works**:
1. Feature-gated (`feature = "spaces"`)
2. Uses mock client by default (production implementation would wire real SpacetimeDB client)
3. Calls `register_spaces_tools(&mut registry, port)` which registers all 4 tools
4. Tools are then available to the agent via the canonical tool harness

**Current state**: Mock-only. Real SpacetimeDB client implementation is not yet wired (would require async-to-sync bridge via `spawn_blocking`).

---

## Summary

The arcan-spaces bridge is a **port-based abstraction layer** that:
1. **Defines a synchronous contract** (`SpacesPort`) matching Arcan's tool execution model
2. **Provides 4 production tools** for channel messaging, DM, listing, and reading
3. **Includes observability middleware** that logs agent activity to AgentLog channels (non-blocking)
4. **Ships with a full mock** for testing without SpacetimeDB
5. **Is feature-gated** and currently uses the mock by default

**Next step for production use**: Implement a `RealSpacesClient: SpacesPort` that wraps the SpacetimeDB SDK's async calls in `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` to satisfy the synchronous trait contract.

Perfect! I now have a complete understanding of the Arcan agent loop. Let me compile the findings.

## Arcan Agent Loop Architecture

I've mapped out the complete agent loop flow and state management. Here's what I found:

---

## 1. Agent Loop Implementation (`arcan-core/src/runtime.rs`)

### Core: The Orchestrator

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/runtime.rs`

The `Orchestrator` is the heart of the agent loop. Key components:

**Structure**:
```rust
pub struct Orchestrator {
    provider: Arc<std::sync::RwLock<Arc<dyn Provider>>>,  // Swappable LLM provider
    tools: ToolRegistry,                                   // All registered tools
    middlewares: Vec<Arc<dyn Middleware>>,                 // Middleware chain
    config: OrchestratorConfig,                            // Max iterations, context config
}
```

**Loop Flow** (lines 215-634):
1. **Iteration Boundary** → Check cancellation flag
2. **Context Compaction** → Trim messages if context budget exceeded
3. **Before Middleware** → Run all `middleware.before_model_call()`
4. **Provider Call** → `provider.complete()` returns `ModelTurn`
5. **After Middleware** → Run all `middleware.after_model_call()`
6. **Process Directives**:
   - `Text` → Append to message history
   - `ToolCall` → Execute via tool registry
     - **Pre-tool middleware** → `middleware.pre_tool_call()`
     - **Tool execution** → `tool.execute()`
     - **State patch** → Apply `result.state_patch` to `AppState`
     - **Post-tool middleware** → `middleware.post_tool_call()`
   - `StatePatch` → Apply directly to state
   - `FinalAnswer` → Stop loop
7. **Stop Condition Check** → `EndTurn`, `NeedsUser`, `Safety`, or `MaxTokens`
8. **Event Emission** → All actions produce `AgentEvent`s via callback

---

## 2. Agent State (`arcan-core/src/state.rs`)

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/state.rs`

**AppState Structure**:
```rust
pub struct AppState {
    pub revision: u64,           // Incremented on every patch
    pub data: Value,             // JSON object holding state
}
```

**State Updates**:
- Tools can return `StatePatch` in their `ToolResult`
- Two patch formats: `JsonPatch` (RFC 6902) or `MergePatch` (RFC 7396)
- State is versioned — `revision` increments on every patch
- Well-known keys: `cwd`, `open_files`, `session_meta`, `budget`, `active_skills`, `mcp_servers`

**Critical Pattern**: State is **reconstructed from event history** on every run. The agent's message history IS the application state.

---

## 3. Tool Registration and Execution

**How Tools are Registered** (`main.rs` lines 461-491):
```rust
let mut registry = ToolRegistry::default();
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(ReadFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(WriteFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(EditFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
// ... more Praxis tools
registry.register(MemoryQueryTool::new(memory_projection));
registry.register(MemoryProposeTool::new(journal.clone()));
```

**Tool Trait** (`runtime.rs` lines 80-83):
```rust
pub trait Tool: Send + Sync {
    fn definition(&self) -> ToolDefinition;
    fn execute(&self, call: &ToolCall, ctx: &ToolContext) -> Result<ToolResult, CoreError>;
}
```

**Tool Execution Flow** (lines 406-502):
1. Middleware `pre_tool_call()` (policy checks)
2. Lookup tool in registry
3. Execute tool → returns `ToolResult`
4. Apply state patch (if present)
5. Middleware `post_tool_call()` (observability)
6. Emit `ToolCallCompleted` event
7. Append result to message history

---

## 4. Middleware Registration and Hooks

**Middleware Trait** (`runtime.rs` lines 85-113):
```rust
pub trait Middleware: Send + Sync {
    fn before_model_call(&self, request: &ProviderRequest) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn after_model_call(&self, request: &ProviderRequest, response: &ModelTurn) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn pre_tool_call(&self, context: &ToolContext, call: &ToolCall) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn post_tool_call(&self, context: &ToolContext, result: &ToolResult) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn on_run_finished(&self, output: &RunOutput) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
}
```

**Middleware Examples**:

1. **LagoPolicyMiddleware** (`arcan-lago/src/policy_middleware.rs`):
   - **Hook**: `pre_tool_call`
   - **Purpose**: Evaluate policy rules, block/allow/gate tools
   - **Approval Flow**: Can pause execution, emit `ApprovalRequested` event, block until resolved

2. **SpacesActivityMiddleware** (`arcan-spaces/src/middleware.rs`):
   - **Hooks**: `post_tool_call`, `on_run_finished`
   - **Purpose**: Log tool activity to distributed Spaces channel
   - **Pattern**: Swallows errors (observability must never block the loop)

**How Middleware is Wired**:
- Middleware is stored in `Orchestrator.middlewares: Vec<Arc<dyn Middleware>>`
- Hooks are called sequentially via `try_for_each()` (lines 636-662)
- **Critical**: Middleware errors STOP the loop (`RunStopReason::BlockedByPolicy`)

---

## 5. Canonical Runtime (aiOS Integration)

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan/src/main.rs` lines 502-541

**KernelRuntime Initialization**:
```rust
let runtime = Arc::new(KernelRuntime::new(
    RuntimeConfig::new(data_dir.to_path_buf()),
    event_store: Arc<dyn EventStorePort>,    // LagoAiosEventStoreAdapter
    provider: Arc<dyn ModelProviderPort>,    // ArcanProviderAdapter (wraps Orchestrator)
    tool_harness: Arc<dyn ToolHarnessPort>, // ArcanHarnessAdapter (wraps ToolRegistry)
    approvals: Arc<dyn ApprovalPort>,        // ArcanApprovalAdapter
    policy_gate: Arc<dyn PolicyGatePort>,    // ArcanPolicyAdapter or AutonomicPolicyAdapter
));
```

**Adapters** (bridge Arcan to aiOS protocol):
- **ArcanProviderAdapter**: Wraps swappable provider handle, translates `ProviderRequest` → aiOS types
- **ArcanHarnessAdapter**: Wraps `ToolRegistry`, implements `ToolHarnessPort`
- **AutonomicPolicyAdapter**: Decorates base policy with Autonomic advisory gating
- **LagoAiosEventStoreAdapter**: Implements `EventStorePort` over Lago journal

**HTTP Entry Point** (`arcand/src/canonical.rs` lines 556-617):
```rust
async fn run_session(session_id, request) {
    let tick = runtime.tick_on_branch(&session_id, &branch, TickInput {
        objective: request.objective,
        proposed_tool: request.proposed_tool,
    }).await?;
    
    // Returns: mode, state, events_emitted, last_sequence
}
```

---

## 6. Event Flow and Streaming

**AgentEvent Types** (`arcan-core/src/protocol.rs` lines 260-352):
- `RunStarted`, `IterationStarted`, `ModelOutput`, `TextDelta`
- `ToolCallRequested`, `ToolCallCompleted`, `ToolCallFailed`
- `StatePatched`, `ContextCompacted`
- `ApprovalRequested`, `ApprovalResolved`
- `RunErrored`, `RunFinished`

**Event Handler Pattern** (lines 244-251, 615):
```rust

    // Callback fires for every event
    // Can emit SSE, update UI, persist to journal, etc.
});
```

**SSE Streaming** (`canonical.rs` lines 727-792):
1. Replay past events from journal (cursor-based)
2. Subscribe to live broadcast channel
3. Stream as Server-Sent Events
4. Supports canonical format + Vercel AI SDK v6 format

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **State is Append-Only**: The agent's message history + event log IS the state. `AppState.data` is reconstructed from patches.
2. **Middleware is Sequential and Blocking**: Any middleware returning `Err()` stops the loop immediately.
3. **Tools are Isolated**: Each tool execution is wrapped in pre/post middleware hooks (policy enforcement, observability).
4. **Approval Gates Pause Execution**: `LagoPolicyMiddleware` can block the loop synchronously until approval is resolved (via oneshot channel).
5. **Swappable Providers**: The provider handle uses `RwLock<Arc<dyn Provider>>` for live switching via HTTP API.
6. **Bridge Pattern**: Arcan adapters translate between Arcan's `Orchestrator` and aiOS's `KernelRuntime` ports.

Perfect! I now have a complete understanding of the Arcan agent loop. Let me compile the findings.

## Arcan Agent Loop Architecture

I've mapped out the complete agent loop flow and state management. Here's what I found:

---

## 1. Agent Loop Implementation (`arcan-core/src/runtime.rs`)

### Core: The Orchestrator

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/runtime.rs`

The `Orchestrator` is the heart of the agent loop. Key components:

**Structure**:
```rust
pub struct Orchestrator {
    provider: Arc<std::sync::RwLock<Arc<dyn Provider>>>,  // Swappable LLM provider
    tools: ToolRegistry,                                   // All registered tools
    middlewares: Vec<Arc<dyn Middleware>>,                 // Middleware chain
    config: OrchestratorConfig,                            // Max iterations, context config
}
```

**Loop Flow** (lines 215-634):
1. **Iteration Boundary** → Check cancellation flag
2. **Context Compaction** → Trim messages if context budget exceeded
3. **Before Middleware** → Run all `middleware.before_model_call()`
4. **Provider Call** → `provider.complete()` returns `ModelTurn`
5. **After Middleware** → Run all `middleware.after_model_call()`
6. **Process Directives**:
   - `Text` → Append to message history
   - `ToolCall` → Execute via tool registry
     - **Pre-tool middleware** → `middleware.pre_tool_call()`
     - **Tool execution** → `tool.execute()`
     - **State patch** → Apply `result.state_patch` to `AppState`
     - **Post-tool middleware** → `middleware.post_tool_call()`
   - `StatePatch` → Apply directly to state
   - `FinalAnswer` → Stop loop
7. **Stop Condition Check** → `EndTurn`, `NeedsUser`, `Safety`, or `MaxTokens`
8. **Event Emission** → All actions produce `AgentEvent`s via callback

---

## 2. Agent State (`arcan-core/src/state.rs`)

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan-core/src/state.rs`

**AppState Structure**:
```rust
pub struct AppState {
    pub revision: u64,           // Incremented on every patch
    pub data: Value,             // JSON object holding state
}
```

**State Updates**:
- Tools can return `StatePatch` in their `ToolResult`
- Two patch formats: `JsonPatch` (RFC 6902) or `MergePatch` (RFC 7396)
- State is versioned — `revision` increments on every patch
- Well-known keys: `cwd`, `open_files`, `session_meta`, `budget`, `active_skills`, `mcp_servers`

**Critical Pattern**: State is **reconstructed from event history** on every run. The agent's message history IS the application state.

---

## 3. Tool Registration and Execution

**How Tools are Registered** (`main.rs` lines 461-491):
```rust
let mut registry = ToolRegistry::default();
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(ReadFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(WriteFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
registry.register(PraxisToolBridge::new(EditFileTool::new(fs_policy.clone())));
// ... more Praxis tools
registry.register(MemoryQueryTool::new(memory_projection));
registry.register(MemoryProposeTool::new(journal.clone()));
```

**Tool Trait** (`runtime.rs` lines 80-83):
```rust
pub trait Tool: Send + Sync {
    fn definition(&self) -> ToolDefinition;
    fn execute(&self, call: &ToolCall, ctx: &ToolContext) -> Result<ToolResult, CoreError>;
}
```

**Tool Execution Flow** (lines 406-502):
1. Middleware `pre_tool_call()` (policy checks)
2. Lookup tool in registry
3. Execute tool → returns `ToolResult`
4. Apply state patch (if present)
5. Middleware `post_tool_call()` (observability)
6. Emit `ToolCallCompleted` event
7. Append result to message history

---

## 4. Middleware Registration and Hooks

**Middleware Trait** (`runtime.rs` lines 85-113):
```rust
pub trait Middleware: Send + Sync {
    fn before_model_call(&self, request: &ProviderRequest) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn after_model_call(&self, request: &ProviderRequest, response: &ModelTurn) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn pre_tool_call(&self, context: &ToolContext, call: &ToolCall) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn post_tool_call(&self, context: &ToolContext, result: &ToolResult) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
    fn on_run_finished(&self, output: &RunOutput) -> Result<(), CoreError>;
}
```

**Middleware Examples**:

1. **LagoPolicyMiddleware** (`arcan-lago/src/policy_middleware.rs`):
   - **Hook**: `pre_tool_call`
   - **Purpose**: Evaluate policy rules, block/allow/gate tools
   - **Approval Flow**: Can pause execution, emit `ApprovalRequested` event, block until resolved

2. **SpacesActivityMiddleware** (`arcan-spaces/src/middleware.rs`):
   - **Hooks**: `post_tool_call`, `on_run_finished`
   - **Purpose**: Log tool activity to distributed Spaces channel
   - **Pattern**: Swallows errors (observability must never block the loop)

**How Middleware is Wired**:
- Middleware is stored in `Orchestrator.middlewares: Vec<Arc<dyn Middleware>>`
- Hooks are called sequentially via `try_for_each()` (lines 636-662)
- **Critical**: Middleware errors STOP the loop (`RunStopReason::BlockedByPolicy`)

---

## 5. Canonical Runtime (aiOS Integration)

**Location**: `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/arcan/crates/arcan/src/main.rs` lines 502-541

**KernelRuntime Initialization**:
```rust
let runtime = Arc::new(KernelRuntime::new(
    RuntimeConfig::new(data_dir.to_path_buf()),
    event_store: Arc<dyn EventStorePort>,    // LagoAiosEventStoreAdapter
    provider: Arc<dyn ModelProviderPort>,    // ArcanProviderAdapter (wraps Orchestrator)
    tool_harness: Arc<dyn ToolHarnessPort>, // ArcanHarnessAdapter (wraps ToolRegistry)
    approvals: Arc<dyn ApprovalPort>,        // ArcanApprovalAdapter
    policy_gate: Arc<dyn PolicyGatePort>,    // ArcanPolicyAdapter or AutonomicPolicyAdapter
));
```

**Adapters** (bridge Arcan to aiOS protocol):
- **ArcanProviderAdapter**: Wraps swappable provider handle, translates `ProviderRequest` → aiOS types
- **ArcanHarnessAdapter**: Wraps `ToolRegistry`, implements `ToolHarnessPort`
- **AutonomicPolicyAdapter**: Decorates base policy with Autonomic advisory gating
- **LagoAiosEventStoreAdapter**: Implements `EventStorePort` over Lago journal

**HTTP Entry Point** (`arcand/src/canonical.rs` lines 556-617):
```rust
async fn run_session(session_id, request) {
    let tick = runtime.tick_on_branch(&session_id, &branch, TickInput {
        objective: request.objective,
        proposed_tool: request.proposed_tool,
    }).await?;
    
    // Returns: mode, state, events_emitted, last_sequence
}
```

---

## 6. Event Flow and Streaming

**AgentEvent Types** (`arcan-core/src/protocol.rs` lines 260-352):
- `RunStarted`, `IterationStarted`, `ModelOutput`, `TextDelta`
- `ToolCallRequested`, `ToolCallCompleted`, `ToolCallFailed`
- `StatePatched`, `ContextCompacted`
- `ApprovalRequested`, `ApprovalResolved`
- `RunErrored`, `RunFinished`

**Event Handler Pattern** (lines 244-251, 615):
```rust
orchestrator.run(input, |event: AgentEvent| {
    // Callback fires for every event
    // Can emit SSE, update UI, persist to journal, etc.
});
```

**SSE Streaming** (`canonical.rs` lines 727-792):
1. Replay past events from journal (cursor-based)
2. Subscribe to live broadcast channel
3. Stream as Server-Sent Events
4. Supports canonical format + Vercel AI SDK v6 format

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **State is Append-Only**: The agent's message history + event log IS the state. `AppState.data` is reconstructed from patches.
2. **Middleware is Sequential and Blocking**: Any middleware returning `Err()` stops the loop immediately.
3. **Tools are Isolated**: Each tool execution is wrapped in pre/post middleware hooks (policy enforcement, observability).
4. **Approval Gates Pause Execution**: `LagoPolicyMiddleware` can block the loop synchronously until approval is resolved (via oneshot channel).
5. **Swappable Providers**: The provider handle uses `RwLock<Arc<dyn Provider>>` for live switching via HTTP API.
6. **Bridge Pattern**: Arcan adapters translate between Arcan's `Orchestrator` and aiOS's `KernelRuntime` ports.

Perfect! I have a comprehensive understanding of the Spaces project. Let me provide you with a complete analysis.

---

## Spaces Project Analysis

### Overview
**Spaces** is a Discord-like distributed agent networking engine built on **SpacetimeDB 2.0**. It provides real-time communication infrastructure for the Agent OS, enabling agents to interact through servers, channels, messages, threads, and direct messaging.

---

## 1. WASM Module Schema (Server-side)

### **11 Tables** (all public)

#### User Tables
1. **UserProfile** - User account data
   - PK: `identity: Identity`
   - Unique: `username: String`
   - Fields: `display_name, avatar_url, bio, is_agent, created_at`

2. **UserPresence** - Online/offline status
   - PK: `identity: Identity`
   - Fields: `online: bool, status_text, last_seen`

#### Server Tables
3. **Server** - Discord-like servers
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Fields: `name, description, icon_url, owner: Identity, created_at`

4. **ServerMember** - Server membership and roles
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `server_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `identity, role: MemberRole, joined_at`

#### Channel Tables
5. **Channel** - Communication channels within servers
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `server_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `name, topic, channel_type: ChannelType, position: u32, created_at`

#### Thread Tables
6. **Thread** - Threaded conversations within channels
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `channel_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `name, creator, created_at, archived: bool, last_message_at`

#### Message Tables
7. **Message** - Main message content
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `channel_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `thread_id, sender, content, message_type: MessageType, created_at, edited_at, reply_to_id`

8. **Reaction** - Emoji reactions on messages
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `message_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `identity, emoji: String`

9. **ChannelReadState** - Read receipts per user+channel
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `identity` (btree)
   - Fields: `channel_id, last_read_msg_id`

#### Direct Messaging Tables
10. **DirectConversation** - 1:1 conversations (normalized participants)
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `participant_a, participant_b, created_at, last_message_at`
    - **Normalization**: `participant_a < participant_b` (lexicographically by hex)

11. **DirectMessage** - Direct message content
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Index: `conversation_id` (btree)
    - Fields: `sender, content, created_at, edited_at, read: bool`

#### Event Tables (Transient)
12. **TypingIndicator** - Real-time typing status
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `channel_id, identity`

13. **SystemNotification** - Server notifications
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `target: Identity, title, body`

---

### **Custom Types** (3 enums)

1. **MemberRole** - 5-tier RBAC hierarchy
   ```rust
   Owner (4) > Admin (3) > Moderator (2) > Member (1) = Agent (1)
   ```

2. **ChannelType** - 4 channel variants
   - `Text` - Standard text chat
   - `Voice` - Voice channels
   - `Announcement` - Read-only announcements
   - `AgentLog` - Agent activity logs

3. **MessageType** - 5 message variants
   - `Text` - User messages
   - `System` - System events
   - `AgentEvent` - Agent actions
   - `Join` - User joined
   - `Leave` - User left

---

### **20+ Reducers** (Server-side mutations)

#### User Reducers
- `set_profile(username, display_name?, avatar_url?, bio?)` - Update user profile
- `register_agent(agent_name, description)` - Convert user to agent

#### Server Management
- `create_server(name, description?)` - Create new server with default #general channel
- `update_server(server_id, name?, description?)` - Admin+ only
- `delete_server(server_id)` - Owner only, cascades to all children
- `join_server(server_id)` - Auto-assigns Member/Agent role
- `leave_server(server_id)` - Owner cannot leave
- `update_member_role(server_id, target_identity, new_role)` - Role hierarchy enforcement

#### Channel Management
- `create_channel(server_id, name, channel_type)` - Admin+ only
- `update_channel(channel_id, name?, topic?)` - Admin+ only
- `delete_channel(channel_id)` - Admin+ only, cascades messages/reactions/threads

#### Thread Management
- `create_thread(channel_id, name, initial_message)` - Creates thread with first message
- `archive_thread(thread_id)` - Creator or admin only

#### Messaging
- `send_message(channel_id, content, thread_id?, reply_to_id?)` - Send text message
- `edit_message(message_id, new_content)` - Owner only
- `delete_message(message_id)` - Owner or admin
- `add_reaction(message_id, emoji)` - Add emoji reaction
- `remove_reaction(message_id, emoji)` - Remove own reaction
- `start_typing(channel_id)` - Transient typing indicator
- `mark_read(channel_id, message_id)` - Update read state

#### Direct Messaging (feature-gated)
- `send_direct_message(recipient, content)` - Finds/creates conversation
- `edit_direct_message(message_id, new_content)` - Owner only
- `delete_direct_message(message_id)` - Owner only
- `mark_dm_read(conversation_id)` - Marks all unread as read

---

### **Lifecycle Hooks** (3)

1. `init` - Creates default "Spaces Hub" server with 3 channels:
   - #general (Text)
   - #agent-logs (AgentLog)
   - #system (Announcement)

2. `client_connected` - Auto-creates UserProfile and UserPresence, auto-joins Spaces Hub

3. `client_disconnected` - Sets `online: false` in UserPresence

---

### **Authorization Module** (`auth.rs`)

Pure helper functions for RBAC enforcement:
- `get_membership(server_id, identity)` - Find membership row
- `get_member_role(server_id, identity)` - Extract role
- `require_member(server_id)` - Guard: must be member
- `require_role(server_id, min_role)` - Guard: must have sufficient role level
- `get_server_for_channel(channel_id)` - Lookup channel's parent server

---

### **Validation Module** (`validation.rs`)

**Pure Rust functions (no SpacetimeDB deps)** - can be tested natively:

#### String Validation
- `validate_username` - 1-32 chars
- `validate_name` - 1-100 chars (server/channel names)
- `validate_message_content` - 1-4000 chars
- `validate_emoji` - Non-empty
- `validate_agent_name` - Non-empty
- `validate_thread_name` - Non-empty
- `validate_dm_not_self` - Cannot DM yourself

#### Role Hierarchy
- `can_modify_role(actor_level, target_level, new_role_level, target_is_owner, new_role_is_owner)`
  - Rules: Cannot modify owner, cannot assign owner, actor must outrank both target and new role

#### State Guards
- `guard_thread_not_archived` - Prevent posting in archived threads
- `guard_thread_channel` - Verify thread belongs to channel
- `guard_not_already_agent` - Prevent duplicate agent registration
- `guard_message_owner` - Only owner can edit
- `guard_delete_permission` - Owner or admin can delete
- `guard_archive_permission` - Creator or admin can archive
- `guard_owner_cannot_leave` - Owner must transfer or delete
- `guard_only_owner_deletes` - Only owner deletes server

**Test Coverage**: 30+ unit tests in `validation.rs` (runs with `cargo test --no-default-features`)

---

## 2. CLI Client Architecture

### **Main Components** (`src/main.rs`)

#### Connection Management
- **Environment Variables**:
  - `SPACETIMEDB_HOST` - Defaults to `https://maincloud.spacetimedb.com`
  - `SPACETIMEDB_DB_NAME` - Defaults to `spaces`
- **Credential Storage**: `.spacetime/spaces` (saved token for persistent identity)
- **Connection Pattern**:
  ```rust
  DbConnection::builder()
      .on_connect(on_connected)
      .on_connect_error(on_connect_error)
      .on_disconnect(on_disconnected)
      .with_token(creds_store().load().ok().flatten())
      .with_database_name(db_name)
      .with_uri(host)
      .build()?
  ```

#### State Management
- **Shared State** (AtomicU64):
  - `CURRENT_SERVER_ID` - Active server context
  - `CURRENT_CHANNEL_ID` - Active channel context
  - `READY` - Subscription applied flag

#### Subscription Model
- **Subscribe to ALL tables** (full materialized view)
- **Callbacks**:
  - `on_sub_applied` - Initial load complete, auto-select first server/channel, print recent messages
  - `on_user_profile_inserted` - Print new user
  - `on_user_presence_updated` - Print online/offline events
  - `on_message_inserted` - Print messages for active channel only
  - `on_server_inserted` - Print new server
  - `on_channel_inserted` - Print new channel
  - `on_direct_message_inserted` (feature-gated) - Print DMs

#### Command Loop
- **Text Input** → Send message to current channel
- **Slash Commands** → Execute CLI commands

---

### **CLI Commands** (21 commands)

#### Navigation
- `/servers` - List all servers
- `/server <id|name>` - Select active server, auto-select first text channel
- `/channels` - List channels in current server
- `/channel <id|name>` - Select active channel, show recent 25 messages
- `/threads` - List active threads in current channel
- `/history [n]` - Show last n messages (default 25)

#### Server Management
- `/create-server <name>` - Create new server
- `/join <server_id>` - Join server
- `/leave <server_id>` - Leave server

#### Channel Management
- `/create-channel <name>` - Create text channel in current server

#### Thread Management
- `/thread <name> | <initial message>` - Create thread with initial message

#### Messaging
- `<any text>` - Send message to current channel
- `/react <msg_id> <emoji>` - React to message

#### User
- `/name <username>` - Set username
- `/agent <name> | <description>` - Register as agent
- `/who` - Show online users
- `/members` - List server members
- `/help` - Show help

#### Direct Messages (feature-gated `dm`)
- `/dm <username> <message>` - Send DM
- `/dms` - List conversations with unread counts
- `/dm-history <conversation_id> [n]` - Show DM history, auto-marks as read

---

### **Client Features**

#### Auto-Selection Flow
1. On subscription applied → auto-select first server
2. Auto-select first text channel in server
3. Print recent 25 messages

#### Real-time Updates
- Messages appear instantly for active channel
- User online/offline events printed
- New servers/channels announced

#### Message Display
- Type prefix: `[SYSTEM]`, `[JOIN]`, `[LEAVE]`, `[AGENT]`, or none
- Thread suffix: `(thread:123)`
- Reply suffix: `(reply to:456)`
- Edit marker: `(edited)`

#### User Display Names
- Prefers `display_name` → `username` → abbreviated Identity hex

---

## 3. CLAUDE.md for Spaces

The CLAUDE.md is **comprehensive and production-ready**:

### Structure
1. **Migration Guide** - SpacetimeDB 1.0 → 2.0 breaking changes reference
2. **Language-Specific Rules** - Links to TypeScript/Rust/C# rule files
3. **Core Concepts** - 5 key principles (transactional reducers, deterministic, subscriptions, auto-inc, ctx.sender)
4. **Feature Checklist** - 5-step implementation pattern (backend tables → reducers → client subscribe → call reducers → render)
5. **Index System** - Automatic indexing, unique naming across module, schema-code coupling warnings
6. **Commands** - `spacetime login/start/publish/generate/logs`
7. **Deployment** - Maincloud defaults, free hosting, dashboard URL pattern
8. **Debugging Checklist** - 5-step troubleshooting flow
9. **Editing Behavior** - Minimal change policy, no hallucinated APIs
10. **Common Mistakes** - 14 documented LLM hallucination patterns with ✅/❌ examples
11. **Rust SDK Reference** - 11 sections covering tables, reducers, indexes, custom types, scheduled tables, client SDK, procedures, logging, commands, requirements

### Key Guidelines
- **DO NOT derive `SpacetimeType` on `#[table]` structs** - The macro handles this
- **Import `Table` trait** - Required for `.insert()`, `.iter()`, `.find()`
- **Tables are methods** - `ctx.db.table()` not `ctx.db.table`
- **Reducers return `Result<(), String>`** - No data return, use tables for reads
- **Update requires full row** - Spread existing row: `..existing`
- **Client SDK is blocking** - Use `spawn_blocking` or dedicated thread if mixing with async
- **CRITICAL**: Client must call `run_threaded()`, `run_async()`, or `frame_tick()` to advance connection

---

## Architecture Summary

### Server-side (WASM Module)
- **Language**: Rust 2021 Edition (SpacetimeDB WASM requirement)
- **Crate**: `spacetimedb/` (cdylib)
- **Deterministic**: No filesystem, network, timers, or external RNG in reducers
- **Pure validation**: Extracted to `validation.rs` for native testing

### Client-side (CLI)
- **Language**: Rust 2024 Edition
- **Crate**: `spaces` (binary)
- **SDK**: `spacetimedb-sdk 2.0.*`
- **Bindings**: Auto-generated via `spacetime generate --lang rust --out-dir src/module_bindings`
- **Blocking I/O**: SDK uses blocking calls - must use `spawn_blocking` if mixing with async

### Key Integration Points
1. **Identity-based auth** - `ctx.sender()` is authenticated principal
2. **Real-time subscriptions** - Client maintains materialized view of all 11 tables
3. **Event-driven callbacks** - Row insert/update/delete callbacks for UI reactivity
4. **RBAC enforcement** - 5-tier role hierarchy with pure validation functions
5. **Cascade deletes** - Server deletion cascades to all channels, messages, reactions, threads
6. **Auto-join pattern** - New users auto-join "Spaces Hub" server on first connection
7. **Normalized DMs** - Participants sorted lexicographically to prevent duplicate conversations
8. **Feature flags** - `dm` feature for direct messaging (requires regenerated bindings)

---

### Files Summary

**WASM Module (spacetimedb/src/):**
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/lib.rs` - Module entry + lifecycle hooks (140 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/tables.rs` - 11 table definitions (179 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/types.rs` - 3 custom enums (42 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/auth.rs` - RBAC helpers (70 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/validation.rs` - Pure validation + tests (458 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/reducers/` - 6 reducer modules:
  - `users.rs` - 72 lines
  - `servers.rs` - 276 lines
  - `channels.rs` - 88 lines
  - `messages.rs` - 205 lines

content.text:
Perfect! I have a comprehensive understanding of the Spaces project. Let me provide you with a complete analysis.

---

## Spaces Project Analysis

### Overview
**Spaces** is a Discord-like distributed agent networking engine built on **SpacetimeDB 2.0**. It provides real-time communication infrastructure for the Agent OS, enabling agents to interact through servers, channels, messages, threads, and direct messaging.

---

## 1. WASM Module Schema (Server-side)

### **11 Tables** (all public)

#### User Tables
1. **UserProfile** - User account data
   - PK: `identity: Identity`
   - Unique: `username: String`
   - Fields: `display_name, avatar_url, bio, is_agent, created_at`

2. **UserPresence** - Online/offline status
   - PK: `identity: Identity`
   - Fields: `online: bool, status_text, last_seen`

#### Server Tables
3. **Server** - Discord-like servers
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Fields: `name, description, icon_url, owner: Identity, created_at`

4. **ServerMember** - Server membership and roles
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `server_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `identity, role: MemberRole, joined_at`

#### Channel Tables
5. **Channel** - Communication channels within servers
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `server_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `name, topic, channel_type: ChannelType, position: u32, created_at`

#### Thread Tables
6. **Thread** - Threaded conversations within channels
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `channel_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `name, creator, created_at, archived: bool, last_message_at`

#### Message Tables
7. **Message** - Main message content
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `channel_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `thread_id, sender, content, message_type: MessageType, created_at, edited_at, reply_to_id`

8. **Reaction** - Emoji reactions on messages
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `message_id` (btree)
   - Fields: `identity, emoji: String`

9. **ChannelReadState** - Read receipts per user+channel
   - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
   - Index: `identity` (btree)
   - Fields: `channel_id, last_read_msg_id`

#### Direct Messaging Tables
10. **DirectConversation** - 1:1 conversations (normalized participants)
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `participant_a, participant_b, created_at, last_message_at`
    - **Normalization**: `participant_a < participant_b` (lexicographically by hex)

11. **DirectMessage** - Direct message content
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Index: `conversation_id` (btree)
    - Fields: `sender, content, created_at, edited_at, read: bool`

#### Event Tables (Transient)
12. **TypingIndicator** - Real-time typing status
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `channel_id, identity`

13. **SystemNotification** - Server notifications
    - PK: `id: u64` (auto_inc)
    - Fields: `target: Identity, title, body`

---

### **Custom Types** (3 enums)

1. **MemberRole** - 5-tier RBAC hierarchy
   ```rust
   Owner (4) > Admin (3) > Moderator (2) > Member (1) = Agent (1)
   ```

2. **ChannelType** - 4 channel variants
   - `Text` - Standard text chat
   - `Voice` - Voice channels
   - `Announcement` - Read-only announcements
   - `AgentLog` - Agent activity logs

3. **MessageType** - 5 message variants
   - `Text` - User messages
   - `System` - System events
   - `AgentEvent` - Agent actions
   - `Join` - User joined
   - `Leave` - User left

---

### **20+ Reducers** (Server-side mutations)

#### User Reducers
- `set_profile(username, display_name?, avatar_url?, bio?)` - Update user profile
- `register_agent(agent_name, description)` - Convert user to agent

#### Server Management
- `create_server(name, description?)` - Create new server with default #general channel
- `update_server(server_id, name?, description?)` - Admin+ only
- `delete_server(server_id)` - Owner only, cascades to all children
- `join_server(server_id)` - Auto-assigns Member/Agent role
- `leave_server(server_id)` - Owner cannot leave
- `update_member_role(server_id, target_identity, new_role)` - Role hierarchy enforcement

#### Channel Management
- `create_channel(server_id, name, channel_type)` - Admin+ only
- `update_channel(channel_id, name?, topic?)` - Admin+ only
- `delete_channel(channel_id)` - Admin+ only, cascades messages/reactions/threads

#### Thread Management
- `create_thread(channel_id, name, initial_message)` - Creates thread with first message
- `archive_thread(thread_id)` - Creator or admin only

#### Messaging
- `send_message(channel_id, content, thread_id?, reply_to_id?)` - Send text message
- `edit_message(message_id, new_content)` - Owner only
- `delete_message(message_id)` - Owner or admin
- `add_reaction(message_id, emoji)` - Add emoji reaction
- `remove_reaction(message_id, emoji)` - Remove own reaction
- `start_typing(channel_id)` - Transient typing indicator
- `mark_read(channel_id, message_id)` - Update read state

#### Direct Messaging (feature-gated)
- `send_direct_message(recipient, content)` - Finds/creates conversation
- `edit_direct_message(message_id, new_content)` - Owner only
- `delete_direct_message(message_id)` - Owner only
- `mark_dm_read(conversation_id)` - Marks all unread as read

---

### **Lifecycle Hooks** (3)

1. `init` - Creates default "Spaces Hub" server with 3 channels:
   - #general (Text)
   - #agent-logs (AgentLog)
   - #system (Announcement)

2. `client_connected` - Auto-creates UserProfile and UserPresence, auto-joins Spaces Hub

3. `client_disconnected` - Sets `online: false` in UserPresence

---

### **Authorization Module** (`auth.rs`)

Pure helper functions for RBAC enforcement:
- `get_membership(server_id, identity)` - Find membership row
- `get_member_role(server_id, identity)` - Extract role
- `require_member(server_id)` - Guard: must be member
- `require_role(server_id, min_role)` - Guard: must have sufficient role level
- `get_server_for_channel(channel_id)` - Lookup channel's parent server

---

### **Validation Module** (`validation.rs`)

**Pure Rust functions (no SpacetimeDB deps)** - can be tested natively:

#### String Validation
- `validate_username` - 1-32 chars
- `validate_name` - 1-100 chars (server/channel names)
- `validate_message_content` - 1-4000 chars
- `validate_emoji` - Non-empty
- `validate_agent_name` - Non-empty
- `validate_thread_name` - Non-empty
- `validate_dm_not_self` - Cannot DM yourself

#### Role Hierarchy
- `can_modify_role(actor_level, target_level, new_role_level, target_is_owner, new_role_is_owner)`
  - Rules: Cannot modify owner, cannot assign owner, actor must outrank both target and new role

#### State Guards
- `guard_thread_not_archived` - Prevent posting in archived threads
- `guard_thread_channel` - Verify thread belongs to channel
- `guard_not_already_agent` - Prevent duplicate agent registration
- `guard_message_owner` - Only owner can edit
- `guard_delete_permission` - Owner or admin can delete
- `guard_archive_permission` - Creator or admin can archive
- `guard_owner_cannot_leave` - Owner must transfer or delete
- `guard_only_owner_deletes` - Only owner deletes server

**Test Coverage**: 30+ unit tests in `validation.rs` (runs with `cargo test --no-default-features`)

---

## 2. CLI Client Architecture

### **Main Components** (`src/main.rs`)

#### Connection Management
- **Environment Variables**:
  - `SPACETIMEDB_HOST` - Defaults to `https://maincloud.spacetimedb.com`
  - `SPACETIMEDB_DB_NAME` - Defaults to `spaces`
- **Credential Storage**: `.spacetime/spaces` (saved token for persistent identity)
- **Connection Pattern**:
  ```rust
  DbConnection::builder()
      .on_connect(on_connected)
      .on_connect_error(on_connect_error)
      .on_disconnect(on_disconnected)
      .with_token(creds_store().load().ok().flatten())
      .with_database_name(db_name)
      .with_uri(host)
      .build()?
  ```

#### State Management
- **Shared State** (AtomicU64):
  - `CURRENT_SERVER_ID` - Active server context
  - `CURRENT_CHANNEL_ID` - Active channel context
  - `READY` - Subscription applied flag

#### Subscription Model
- **Subscribe to ALL tables** (full materialized view)
- **Callbacks**:
  - `on_sub_applied` - Initial load complete, auto-select first server/channel, print recent messages
  - `on_user_profile_inserted` - Print new user
  - `on_user_presence_updated` - Print online/offline events
  - `on_message_inserted` - Print messages for active channel only
  - `on_server_inserted` - Print new server
  - `on_channel_inserted` - Print new channel
  - `on_direct_message_inserted` (feature-gated) - Print DMs

#### Command Loop
- **Text Input** → Send message to current channel
- **Slash Commands** → Execute CLI commands

---

### **CLI Commands** (21 commands)

#### Navigation
- `/servers` - List all servers
- `/server <id|name>` - Select active server, auto-select first text channel
- `/channels` - List channels in current server
- `/channel <id|name>` - Select active channel, show recent 25 messages
- `/threads` - List active threads in current channel
- `/history [n]` - Show last n messages (default 25)

#### Server Management
- `/create-server <name>` - Create new server
- `/join <server_id>` - Join server
- `/leave <server_id>` - Leave server

#### Channel Management
- `/create-channel <name>` - Create text channel in current server

#### Thread Management
- `/thread <name> | <initial message>` - Create thread with initial message

#### Messaging
- `<any text>` - Send message to current channel
- `/react <msg_id> <emoji>` - React to message

#### User
- `/name <username>` - Set username
- `/agent <name> | <description>` - Register as agent
- `/who` - Show online users
- `/members` - List server members
- `/help` - Show help

#### Direct Messages (feature-gated `dm`)
- `/dm <username> <message>` - Send DM
- `/dms` - List conversations with unread counts
- `/dm-history <conversation_id> [n]` - Show DM history, auto-marks as read

---

### **Client Features**

#### Auto-Selection Flow
1. On subscription applied → auto-select first server
2. Auto-select first text channel in server
3. Print recent 25 messages

#### Real-time Updates
- Messages appear instantly for active channel
- User online/offline events printed
- New servers/channels announced

#### Message Display
- Type prefix: `[SYSTEM]`, `[JOIN]`, `[LEAVE]`, `[AGENT]`, or none
- Thread suffix: `(thread:123)`
- Reply suffix: `(reply to:456)`
- Edit marker: `(edited)`

#### User Display Names
- Prefers `display_name` → `username` → abbreviated Identity hex

---

## 3. CLAUDE.md for Spaces

The CLAUDE.md is **comprehensive and production-ready**:

### Structure
1. **Migration Guide** - SpacetimeDB 1.0 → 2.0 breaking changes reference
2. **Language-Specific Rules** - Links to TypeScript/Rust/C# rule files
3. **Core Concepts** - 5 key principles (transactional reducers, deterministic, subscriptions, auto-inc, ctx.sender)
4. **Feature Checklist** - 5-step implementation pattern (backend tables → reducers → client subscribe → call reducers → render)
5. **Index System** - Automatic indexing, unique naming across module, schema-code coupling warnings
6. **Commands** - `spacetime login/start/publish/generate/logs`
7. **Deployment** - Maincloud defaults, free hosting, dashboard URL pattern
8. **Debugging Checklist** - 5-step troubleshooting flow
9. **Editing Behavior** - Minimal change policy, no hallucinated APIs
10. **Common Mistakes** - 14 documented LLM hallucination patterns with ✅/❌ examples
11. **Rust SDK Reference** - 11 sections covering tables, reducers, indexes, custom types, scheduled tables, client SDK, procedures, logging, commands, requirements

### Key Guidelines
- **DO NOT derive `SpacetimeType` on `#[table]` structs** - The macro handles this
- **Import `Table` trait** - Required for `.insert()`, `.iter()`, `.find()`
- **Tables are methods** - `ctx.db.table()` not `ctx.db.table`
- **Reducers return `Result<(), String>`** - No data return, use tables for reads
- **Update requires full row** - Spread existing row: `..existing`
- **Client SDK is blocking** - Use `spawn_blocking` or dedicated thread if mixing with async
- **CRITICAL**: Client must call `run_threaded()`, `run_async()`, or `frame_tick()` to advance connection

---

## Architecture Summary

### Server-side (WASM Module)
- **Language**: Rust 2021 Edition (SpacetimeDB WASM requirement)
- **Crate**: `spacetimedb/` (cdylib)
- **Deterministic**: No filesystem, network, timers, or external RNG in reducers
- **Pure validation**: Extracted to `validation.rs` for native testing

### Client-side (CLI)
- **Language**: Rust 2024 Edition
- **Crate**: `spaces` (binary)
- **SDK**: `spacetimedb-sdk 2.0.*`
- **Bindings**: Auto-generated via `spacetime generate --lang rust --out-dir src/module_bindings`
- **Blocking I/O**: SDK uses blocking calls - must use `spawn_blocking` if mixing with async

### Key Integration Points
1. **Identity-based auth** - `ctx.sender()` is authenticated principal
2. **Real-time subscriptions** - Client maintains materialized view of all 11 tables
3. **Event-driven callbacks** - Row insert/update/delete callbacks for UI reactivity
4. **RBAC enforcement** - 5-tier role hierarchy with pure validation functions
5. **Cascade deletes** - Server deletion cascades to all channels, messages, reactions, threads
6. **Auto-join pattern** - New users auto-join "Spaces Hub" server on first connection
7. **Normalized DMs** - Participants sorted lexicographically to prevent duplicate conversations
8. **Feature flags** - `dm` feature for direct messaging (requires regenerated bindings)

---

### Files Summary

**WASM Module (spacetimedb/src/):**
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/lib.rs` - Module entry + lifecycle hooks (140 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/tables.rs` - 11 table definitions (179 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/types.rs` - 3 custom enums (42 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/auth.rs` - RBAC helpers (70 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/validation.rs` - Pure validation + tests (458 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/spacetimedb/src/reducers/` - 6 reducer modules:
  - `users.rs` - 72 lines
  - `servers.rs` - 276 lines
  - `channels.rs` - 88 lines
  - `messages.rs` - 205 lines
  - `threads.rs` - 73 lines
  - `direct_messages.rs` - 161 lines

**CLI Client (src/):**
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/src/main.rs` - Full client implementation (887 lines)
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/src/module_bindings/mod.rs` - Generated bindings (1320 lines)

**Documentation:**
- `/Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/spaces/CLAUDE.md` - Comprehensive SpacetimeDB Rust rules (771 lines)

---

This is a **production-quality, feature-complete Discord clone** ready for distributed agent communication. All tables are public, all reducers are well-validated, pure logic is testable, and the CLI provides a fully functional chat interface.

Here's the full architectural picture of how Spaces works and how it integrates with the agent loop.

---

## Spaces: The Distributed Networking Layer

### What It Is

Spaces is a **Discord-like real-time communication fabric** built on SpacetimeDB 2.0. It's where agents talk to each other, to humans, and leave observable traces of their activity. Two components:

1. **WASM Module** (server-side, Rust 2021) — runs inside SpacetimeDB, handles all state and mutations
2. **CLI Client** (Rust 2024) — connects via SDK, subscribes to all tables, provides interactive chat

### Data Model (11 Tables)

```
Server (name, owner, description)
  └── Channel (name, type, topic, position)
        ├── Message (sender, content, type, thread_id, reply_to_id)
        │     └── Reaction (emoji, identity)
        ├── Thread (name, creator, archived)
        └── ChannelReadState (identity, last_read_msg_id)

UserProfile (identity, username, display_name, is_agent)
UserPresence (identity, online, status_text, last_seen)

DirectConversation (participant_a, participant_b)  // normalized: a < b
  └── DirectMessage (sender, content, read)
```

### Channel Types (4)

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **Text** | Standard chat |
| **Voice** | Voice channels (placeholder) |
| **Announcement** | Read-only broadcast |
| **AgentLog** | Agent activity traces — this is the integration point |

### RBAC (5 Tiers)

```
Owner (4) > Admin (3) > Moderator (2) > Member (1) = Agent (1)
```

Agents join as `Member` role with `is_agent: true` on their profile. They have the same permissions as members but are identifiable as non-human.

### Lifecycle

On `init`, SpacetimeDB creates a default "Spaces Hub" server with 3 channels: `#general`, `#agent-logs`, `#system`. When any client connects, it auto-creates a `UserProfile` + `UserPresence` and joins the Hub.

### 20+ Reducers

All mutations are transactional SpacetimeDB reducers — `send_message`, `create_server`, `join_server`, `update_member_role`, `create_thread`, `send_direct_message`, etc. Each reducer validates via pure functions in `validation.rs` (30+ unit tests) and enforces RBAC via `auth.rs`.

---

## The arcan-spaces Bridge

This is the integration layer. It has 4 parts:

### 1. `SpacesPort` Trait (the abstraction)

```rust
pub trait SpacesPort: Send + Sync {
    fn send_message(&self, channel_id: u64, content: &str,
                    thread_id: Option<u64>, reply_to_id: Option<u64>)
                    -> Result<SpacesMessage, SpacesBridgeError>;
    fn list_channels(&self, server_id: u64)
                    -> Result<Vec<SpacesChannel>, SpacesBridgeError>;
    fn read_messages(&self, channel_id: u64, limit: usize,
                     before_id: Option<u64>)
                    -> Result<Vec<SpacesMessage>, SpacesBridgeError>;
    fn send_dm(&self, recipient: &str, content: &str)
                    -> Result<SpacesDirectMessage, SpacesBridgeError>;
}
```

Key design decision: **synchronous**. Arcan's `Tool::execute()` is sync. The SpacetimeDB SDK is blocking anyway (uses blocking I/O). When a real SDK adapter is built, it'll just call the SDK directly — no async bridging needed.

The port defines its own mirror types (`SpacesChannel`, `SpacesMessage`, etc.) so there's **no compile-time dependency on the SpacetimeDB SDK**. This keeps the bridge decoupled.

### 2. Tools (4 agent-facing tools)

| Tool | Read-only | What it does |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| `spaces_list_channels` | yes | List channels in a server (default: server 1) |
| `spaces_read_messages` | yes | Read messages from a channel (limit, pagination) |
| `spaces_send_message` | no | Send a message to a channel (threading, replies) |
| `spaces_send_dm` | no | Send a direct message to a user by identity hex |

These implement Arcan's `Tool` trait. The agent sees them in its tool catalog and can call them like any other tool. Content validation enforces max 4000 chars.

### 3. Middleware (`SpacesActivityMiddleware`)

```rust
impl Middleware for SpacesActivityMiddleware {
    fn post_tool_call(&self, ctx, result) {
        // → sends "[agent] Tool 'read_file' completed" to #agent-logs
    }
    fn on_run_finished(&self, output) {
        // → sends "[agent] Run 'run_xyz' finished: EndTurn (iterations: 3)"
    }
}
```

This is the **observability** integration. Every tool call and every run completion gets logged to a Spaces AgentLog channel. **Critical invariant**: port errors are swallowed — observability must never block the agent loop.

### 4. Mock Hub (for testing)

`MockSpacesClient` implements `SpacesPort` with in-memory state. Pre-seeded with `#general`, `#agent-logs`, `#system`. Supports error injection. Currently used as the default in the arcan binary.

### How It's Wired (feature-gated)

In `arcan/src/main.rs`:
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "spaces")]
{
    let spaces_port: Arc<dyn SpacesPort> =
        Arc::new(MockSpacesClient::default_hub());
    register_spaces_tools(&mut registry, spaces_port);
}
```

---

## How It Fits Into the Agent Loop

Here's the Arcan agent loop with Spaces integration points marked:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Orchestrator.run()                              │
│                                                 │
│  1. Check cancellation                          │
│  2. Context compaction (trim if over budget)    │
│  3. middleware.before_model_call()              │
│  4. provider.complete() → ModelTurn             │
│  5. middleware.after_model_call()               │
│  6. Process directives:                         │
│     ├── Text → append to history                │
│     ├── ToolCall:                               │
│     │   ├── middleware.pre_tool_call()           │
│     │   │   └── Policy check (Lago, Autonomic)  │
│     │   ├── tool.execute()  ◄── SPACES TOOLS    │
│     │   │   └── spaces_send_message             │
│     │   │   └── spaces_read_messages            │
│     │   │   └── spaces_list_channels            │
│     │   │   └── spaces_send_dm                  │
│     │   ├── Apply state_patch (if any)          │
│     │   ├── middleware.post_tool_call()          │
│     │   │   └── SpacesActivityMiddleware ◄──────│
│     │   │       logs to #agent-logs             │
│     │   └── Emit ToolCallCompleted event        │
│     ├── StatePatch → apply to AppState          │
│     └── FinalAnswer → stop loop                 │
│  7. Check stop condition                        │
│  8. Emit events → SSE stream + journal          │
│                                                 │
│  On run finish:                                 │
│     middleware.on_run_finished()                 │
│     └── SpacesActivityMiddleware ◄──────────────│
│         logs run summary to #agent-logs         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Impact on Agent State

Currently, **Spaces tools return `state_patch: None`** — they don't mutate `AppState`. The agent calls them, gets JSON results, and uses those results in its reasoning. This is the right default: Spaces is an I/O channel, not a state mutation.

However, there are natural extension points where Spaces *could* affect agent state:

| Extension | How | State Impact |
|-----------|-----|-------------|
| **Active channel tracking** | Tool returns `state_patch` setting `appstate.spaces.current_channel` | Agent remembers which channel it's in |
| **Unread count** | Middleware polls read state, patches `appstate.spaces.unread` | Agent can prioritize channels |
| **Multi-agent coordination** | Agent reads instructions from other agents via `read_messages` | Indirect state influence through reasoning |
| **Identity** | Store agent's SpacetimeDB identity in state | Persistent identity across runs |

### How the Event Flow Works End-to-End

```
Human sends "tell the team about the deployment" via /chat
  │
  ▼
Arcan receives → reconstructs state from Lago journal
  │
  ▼
LLM sees spaces_send_message in tool catalog
  │
  ▼
LLM decides: ToolCall { name: "spaces_send_message",
                         args: { channel_id: 1, content: "Deployment complete..." } }
  │
  ▼
pre_tool_call: LagoPolicyMiddleware checks policy → ALLOW
pre_tool_call: AutonomicPolicyAdapter checks gating → ALLOW
  │
  ▼
SpacesSendMessageTool.execute()
  → port.send_message(1, "Deployment complete...", None, None)
  → SpacetimeDB reducer: send_message()
  → Message inserted, real-time subscription fires
  → Other connected agents/humans see it instantly
  │
  ▼
post_tool_call: SpacesActivityMiddleware
  → port.send_message(agent_log_channel, "[agent] Tool 'spaces_send_message' completed")
  │
  ▼
ToolResult { content: {"id": 42, "channel_id": 1, ...}, state_patch: None }
  │
  ▼
Appended to message history → LLM sees result → continues reasoning
  │
  ▼
RunFinished → SpacesActivityMiddleware logs summary to #agent-logs
All events persisted to Lago journal (replayable)
```

---

## What's Real vs. What's Pending

| Component | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| SpacetimeDB WASM module (11 tables, 20+ reducers, RBAC) | **Done** |
| CLI client (26 commands, real-time subscriptions) | **Done** |
| `SpacesPort` trait abstraction | **Done** |
| 4 Arcan tools (send, read, list, DM) | **Done** |
| `SpacesActivityMiddleware` (observability) | **Done** |
| Mock hub for testing (18 tests) | **Done** |
| Feature-gated registration in arcan binary | **Done** |
| **Real SpacetimeDB SDK adapter** (`impl SpacesPort`) | **Not done** — currently mock-only |
| Spaces reducer tests | **Not done** — 0 tests in WASM module (validation tests exist in `validation.rs` but only run natively) |
| State patches from Spaces tools | **Not done** — tools return `state_patch: None` |
| Multi-agent coordination protocols | **Not done** — no convention for how agents use channels to coordinate |

### The Key Missing Piece

The real `SpacesPort` implementation that connects to a live SpacetimeDB instance. It would look roughly like:

```rust
struct SpacetimeDbClient {
    conn: DbConnection,  // SpacetimeDB SDK connection
}

impl SpacesPort for SpacetimeDbClient {
    fn send_message(&self, channel_id: u64, content: &str, ...) -> Result<...> {
        // SDK is blocking — call reducer directly
        self.conn.reducers().send_message(channel_id, content, ...)?;
        // Read back the inserted message from local cache
        // (SpacetimeDB maintains a client-side materialized view)
    }
}
```

No `spawn_blocking` needed here because the SpacetimeDB Rust SDK is already blocking. The `SpacesPort` trait is synchronous by design to match this.

Arcan agent runtime with streaming and tool execution

Usage: arcan [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Commands:

  run     Send a single message and print the response (non-interactive)
  config  Manage persistent configuration
  status  Show daemon status, provider, model, and session info
  stop    Stop the running daemon
  login   Authenticate with an LLM provider via OAuth
  logout  Remove stored OAuth credentials for a provider
  api     Open the interactive API docs (Scalar UI) in the browser
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --data-dir <DATA_DIR>            Data directory for journal and blob storage [default: .arcan]
      --port <PORT>                    HTTP listen port
      --console-dir <CONSOLE_DIR>      Serve console assets from a local directory (dev mode)
      --provider <PROVIDER>            LLM provider (anthropic, openai, ollama, mock)
      --model <MODEL>                  Model name override

```

The binary uses **Clap** with `#[derive(Parser)]` and `#[derive(Subcommand)]` for CLI parsing. The `Cli` struct (not shown in first 100 lines) holds the global options (`--data-dir`, `--port`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--autonomic-url`, `--console-dir`) and the `Commands` enum defines the subcommands (`serve`, `chat`, `run`, `config`, `status`, `stop`, `login`, `logout`, `api`).

---

**5. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY**

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set: no
```

The Anthropic API key is **not set** in the current environment. Without it, you can use `--provider mock` or `--provider ollama` (if Ollama is running locally).

58834 /bin/zsh -c ... cargo run -p arcan -- run "What is 2 + 2? Answer in one word." --session autonomic-test
58845 /Users/broomva/broomva.tech/life/.target/debug/arcan run What is 2 + 2? Answer in one word. --session autonomic-test
```

- **Provider**: `ollama`
- **Model**: `gpt-oss:20b`
- **Autonomic URL**: `http://127.0.0.1:3002` (advisory gating enabled)
- **Subcommand**: `serve`

There is also a one-shot `arcan run` command in progress (PIDs 58834/58845) running a test query against the `autonomic-test` session.

| **`grep`** | Search file contents for a regex pattern. | `glob: string`, `pattern: string`, `max_matches?: number`, `path?: string` |

| **`memory_propose`** | Suggest new memory entries for future use. | `entries: array`, `scope: string` |
| **`memory_query`** | Retrieve memory entries for a given scope. | `scope: string`, `limit: number` |
| **`read_file`** | Read a file and return its contents with line metadata. | `path: string` |
| **`read_memory`** | Read a piece of persistent memory by key. | `key: string` |
| **`write_file`** | Overwrite a file with new content. | `path: string`, `content: string` |
| **`write_memory`** | Persist a markdown value under a memory key. | `key: string`, `content: string` |

echo "=== ALL SESSION TOOL REPORTS ==="
for session_dir in .arcan/sessions/*/; do
  session=$(basename "$session_dir")
  runs_dir="$session_dir/tools/runs"
  if [ -d "$runs_dir" ]; then
    for f in "$runs_dir"/*/report.json; do
      [ -f "$f" ] && python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('$f'))
out = d['outcome'].get('output', {})
summary = ''
if isinstance(out, dict):
    if 'content' in out:
        summary = f' content=\"{out[\"content\"][:60]}\"'
    elif 'id' in out:
        summary = f' id={out[\"id\"]}'
elif isinstance(out, list):
    summary = f' items={len(out)}'
print(f'  [{session}] {d[\"tool_name\"]}: {d[\"outcome\"][\"status\"]}{summary} ({d[\"duration_ms\"]}ms)')
" 2>/dev/null
    done
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== AUTONOMIC GATING STATUS ==="
for session_dir in .arcan/sessions/*/; do
  session=$(basename "$session_dir")
  gating=$(curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:3002/gating/$session" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$gating" ]; then
    mode=$(echo "$gating" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['profile']['economic']['economic_mode'])" 2>/dev/null)
    side=$(echo "$gating" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('allow' if d['profile']['operational']['allow_side_effects'] else 'deny')" 2>/dev/null)
    echo "  [$session] mode=$mode side_effects=$side"
  fi
done

content.text:
Everything is working. Here's the full validation summary:

---

## Full Stack Validation Report

### Daemon Health

| Service | URL | Status | Version |
|---------|-----|--------|---------|
| **Arcan** | `localhost:3000` | `ok` | `0.2.1` |
| **Autonomic** | `127.0.0.1:3002` | `ok` | `0.1.0` |

Config: `--provider ollama --model gpt-oss:20b --autonomic-url http://127.0.0.1:3002` with `--features spaces`

### Tool Execution Results

| Session | Tool | Status |
|---------|------|--------|
| `spaces-list-test` | `spaces_list_channels` | **success** — returned 3 channels (general, agent-logs, system) |
| `spaces-send-test` | `spaces_send_message` | **success** — sent to channel 1, got message id=100 |
| `spaces-read-test` | `spaces_read_messages` | **success** — read back the sent message from channel 1 |
| `spaces-e2e-test` | `spaces_list_channels` | **success** — 3 channels returned |
| `autonomic-tool-test` | `read_file` | **success** |
| `autonomic-tool-test` | `list_dir` | **success** |
| `e2e-test` | `bash` | **success** |

### Autonomic Gating

All 8 sessions: **`mode=sovereign`, `side_effects=True`** — Autonomic is advising full operational mode with no restrictions.

### Data Flow Verified

1. `spaces_list_channels` → returned mock hub's 3 default channels (general/text, agent-logs/agent_log, system/announcement)
2. `spaces_send_message` → wrote "Arcan spaces integration test - hello world" to channel 1, got back structured response with id, sender, timestamp
3. `spaces_read_messages` → read back the same message from channel 1, confirming the mock hub's state persists within a session

### What's Working
- Arcan agent loop (Ollama gpt-oss:20b provider)
- Tool discovery (16 tools including 4 Spaces tools)
- Spaces tools execute correctly through the mock hub
- Autonomic advisory gating responds for all sessions (sovereign mode, fail-open)
- Lago journal persistence (`.arcan/journal.redb`, session directories with manifests, tool reports)
- The `run` subcommand correctly displays tool call status (`[tool: X] OK`)

### Note on Output
The `run` subcommand shows tool call status lines (`[tool: spaces_send_message] OK`) but the LLM's follow-up text response was sometimes empty. This is a model behavior issue with `gpt-oss:20b` — it calls the tools correctly but sometimes doesn't produce a textual summary afterward. The tool execution itself is confirmed successful via the persisted `report.json` files.
